# AI Infrastructure Research Papers

This document collects important research papers and academic publications
related to AI infrastructure, LLM serving, distributed training, and
cloud-native AI systems. Papers are organized by topic for easy reference.

**Note:** Some content may be generated by AI tools. Please verify information
before using in production environments.

## LLM Inference and Serving

### vLLM: Efficient Memory Management for LLM Serving

**Paper**: [Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with
PagedAttention](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.06180)

**Authors**: Woosuk Kwon, Zhuohan Li, Siyuan Zhuang, Ying Sheng, Lianmin
Zheng, Cody Hao Yu, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Hao Zhang, Ion Stoica

**Published**: September 2023

**Abstract**: High-throughput serving of large language models (LLMs) requires
batching sufficiently many requests at a time. However, existing systems waste
60%-80% of memory due to fragmentation and inefficient memory sharing. This
paper introduces PagedAttention, an attention algorithm inspired by the classic
virtual memory and paging techniques in operating systems. PagedAttention
allows storing continuous keys and values in non-contiguous memory space,
enabling flexible management of KV cache. We build vLLM, an LLM serving system
that achieves near-zero waste in KV cache memory and flexible sharing of KV
cache across sequences. vLLM improves the throughput of LLM serving by
2-4× compared to state-of-the-art systems while preserving model accuracy.

**Key Contributions**:

- PagedAttention algorithm for efficient KV cache management
- Memory-efficient batching and scheduling
- Flexible KV cache sharing across requests
- 2-4× throughput improvement over existing systems

**Related Projects**:

- [`vLLM`](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm): High-throughput LLM serving
  engine
- [`AIBrix`](https://github.com/vllm-project/aibrix): Cloud-native LLM
  inference infrastructure

**See Also**: [Inference Overview](./inference/README.md) |
[Caching Strategies](./inference/caching.md) |
[Model Lifecycle](./inference/model-lifecycle.md)

### AIBrix: Cloud-Native LLM Inference Infrastructure

**Paper**: [AIBrix: A Kubernetes-Native Platform for Building and Managing
High-Performance LLM Inference Infrastructure](https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.03648)

**Authors**: Hongxin Li, Yu Cao, Qiaoling Chen, Yunfei Ding, et al.

**Published**: April 2025

**Abstract**: AIBrix is an open-source, cloud-native solution optimized for
deploying, managing, and scaling large language model (LLM) inference in
enterprise environments. It provides essential building blocks for constructing
scalable GenAI inference infrastructure with features including high-density
LoRA management, LLM-aware gateway routing, app-tailored autoscaling,
distributed inference, and cost-efficient heterogeneous serving.

**Key Contributions**:

- Cloud-native architecture for LLM inference at scale
- High-density LoRA adapter management and dynamic switching
- LLM-aware load balancing and intelligent routing
- Distributed KV cache for cross-engine sharing
- Cost-efficient heterogeneous GPU serving with SLO guarantees
- Prefill-Decode disaggregation support

**Project Resources**:

- **GitHub**: [vllm-project/aibrix](https://github.com/vllm-project/aibrix)
- **Documentation**: [aibrix.readthedocs.io](https://aibrix.readthedocs.io/)
- **Blog**: [aibrix.github.io](https://aibrix.github.io/)

**See Also**: [AIBrix Introduction](./inference/aibrix.md) |
[Inference Overview](./inference/README.md) |
[P/D Disaggregation](./inference/pd-disaggregation.md)

## Distributed Training

### ByteRobust: Training Large-Scale AI Models with Fault Tolerance

**Paper**: [ByteRobust: Efficient and Effective Fault Tolerance for
Distributed Training at ByteDance Scale](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.16293)

**Authors**: ByteDance AI Infrastructure Team

**Published**: September 2025

**Abstract**: ByteRobust presents a comprehensive fault tolerance framework for
training large-scale AI models at production scale. The paper addresses the
critical challenges of hardware failures, network instability, and resource
contention in distributed training environments. ByteRobust introduces novel
techniques for rapid failure detection, efficient checkpoint management, and
automatic recovery strategies that minimize training interruption and maximize
GPU utilization.

**Key Contributions**:

- Fast failure detection and automatic recovery mechanisms
- Efficient checkpoint and restore strategies
- Dynamic resource reallocation during failures
- Minimal training interruption with sub-minute recovery time
- Production-validated at ByteDance scale

**Related Topics**:

- Distributed training fault tolerance
- Checkpoint/restore strategies for GPU workloads
- Production ML infrastructure at scale
- Hardware failure detection and recovery

**See Also**: [Training Overview](./training/README.md) |
[Parallelism Strategies](./training/parallelism.md) |
[Pre-Training](./training/pre-training.md)

## Networking and Communication

### DRANet: Kubernetes Network Driver with DRA

**Paper**: [The Kubernetes Network Driver Model: A Composable Architecture for
High-Performance Networking](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.23628)

**Authors**: Antonio Ojea (Google), et al.

**Published**: June 2025

**Conference**: IEEE 50th Conference on Local Computer Networks (LCN) 2025

**Abstract**: DRANet introduces a new network driver model for Kubernetes based
on Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA). The paper addresses the limitations of
traditional Kubernetes networking by providing topology-aware network resource
allocation, support for high-performance networking technologies (RDMA,
GPU-Direct, SR-IOV), and seamless integration with AI/ML workloads. DRANet
enables fine-grained control over network resources and optimizes network
performance for distributed training and inference.

**Key Contributions**:

- DRA-based network driver architecture for Kubernetes
- Topology-aware network resource allocation (NUMA, PCIe, NVLink)
- Support for high-performance networking (RDMA, GPU-Direct, SR-IOV)
- Optimized for AI/ML, HPC, and Telco workloads
- Composable network resource management

**Project Resources**:

- **GitHub**: [kubernetes-sigs/dranet](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/dranet)
- **KEP**: Kubernetes Enhancement Proposal for DRA Network Driver

**See Also**: [DRA Guide](./kubernetes/dra.md) |
[Kubernetes Overview](./kubernetes/README.md) |
[DRANet Blog Post (中文)](./blog/2025-12-17/dranet-kubernetes-network-driver_zh.md)

---

### Dragonfly: Large-Scale P2P File Distribution

**Paper**: [Dragonfly: Cloud-Native P2P File Distribution System at Scale](
https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/11152005)

**Authors**: Dragonfly Community

**Published**: IEEE Conference Publication

**Abstract**: Dragonfly is a cloud-native, P2P-based image and file
distribution system designed to improve the efficiency of large-scale container
image distribution in cloud environments. The paper presents Dragonfly's
architecture, which leverages peer-to-peer technology to reduce registry
bandwidth consumption, accelerate image pulls, and improve overall system
reliability in large Kubernetes clusters.

**Key Contributions**:

- P2P-based container image distribution at scale
- Reduced registry bandwidth consumption (up to 90% reduction)
- Faster image pull times for large container images
- Support for AI/ML model distribution and caching
- CNCF Incubating project with production deployments

**Project Resources**:

- **GitHub**: [dragonflyoss/dragonfly](https://github.com/dragonflyoss/dragonfly)
- **Documentation**: [d7y.io](https://d7y.io/)
- **CNCF Status**: Incubating

**Use Cases**:

- Container image distribution in large Kubernetes clusters
- AI model distribution and caching
- Large file distribution across data centers
- Edge computing and CDN scenarios

**See Also**: [Model Lifecycle](./inference/model-lifecycle.md) |
[Kubernetes Overview](./kubernetes/README.md)

## Caching and Memory Management

### LMCache: KV Cache Sharing for LLM Inference

**Paper**: [LMCache: Efficient KV Cache Sharing Across LLM Inference Engines](
https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.09665)

**Authors**: LMCache Research Team

**Published**: October 2025

**Abstract**: LMCache presents a novel approach to KV cache management in LLM
inference by enabling efficient cache sharing across multiple inference engines
and requests. The paper addresses the memory bottleneck in LLM serving by
introducing a distributed KV cache architecture that allows cache reuse across
different models, LoRA adapters, and inference instances. LMCache significantly
reduces memory overhead and improves throughput in multi-tenant LLM serving
environments.

**Key Contributions**:

- Distributed KV cache architecture for cross-engine sharing
- Cache reuse across models, adapters, and inference instances
- Reduced memory overhead in multi-tenant environments
- Improved throughput and reduced latency
- Integration with popular inference engines (vLLM, TGI, SGLang)

**Project Resources**:

- **GitHub**: [LMCache/LMCache](https://github.com/LMCache/LMCache)
- **Integration**: Works with vLLM, TGI, SGLang, and other engines

**Use Cases**:

- Multi-tenant LLM serving
- LoRA adapter management and switching
- Prefill-Decode disaggregation
- Long-context inference optimization

**See Also**: [Caching Strategies](./inference/caching.md) |
[P/D Disaggregation](./inference/pd-disaggregation.md) |
[LoRA Guide](./inference/lora.md)

## Test Time Scaling and Reasoning

### Test Time Scaling Papers Collection

This section collects papers related to test-time scaling, reasoning models,
and "thinking" mechanisms in large language models. Specific arXiv links for
these papers are not yet available; they are based on a Twitter/X discussion
and may be preprints or upcoming publications.

#### Core Papers

1. **DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement
   Learning**
   - Focus: Simple test-time scaling and reasoning
   - Key Topic: What is "Thinking"?
   - Approach: Reinforcement learning for reasoning capability
   - Status: Paper reference pending specific arXiv link

2. **Controlling How Long A Reasoning Model Thinks With Reinforcement Learning**
   - Focus: Efficient Reasoning
   - Key Topic: Thinking as a budget constraint
   - Approach: RL-based control of reasoning depth
   - Status: Paper reference pending specific arXiv link

3. **Scalable Chain of Thoughts via Elastic Reasoning**
   - Focus: Efficient and scalable reasoning
   - Key Topic: Elastic reasoning mechanisms
   - Approach: Adaptive chain-of-thought scaling
   - Status: Paper reference pending specific arXiv link

#### Analysis Papers

4. **(How) Do reasoning models reason?**
   - Focus: Understanding reasoning mechanisms
   - Key Topic: Analysis of reasoning behavior
   - Status: Paper reference pending specific arXiv link

5. **DeepSeek-R1 Thoughtology: Let's Think About Thinking**
   - Focus: Deep analysis of reasoning processes
   - Key Topic: Thoughtology and reasoning patterns
   - Status: Paper reference pending specific arXiv link

**Reference**: [Twitter/X Discussion by @dongxi_nlp](
https://x.com/dongxi_nlp/status/2003574127211479442)

**Note**: This collection is based on a research discussion. Specific paper
links will be added when the papers are published or preprints become available
on arXiv.

**Related Topics**:

- Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning
- Test-time compute scaling
- Reasoning model evaluation
- Model interpretability

**See Also**: [Model Architectures](./inference/model-architectures.md) |

## Contributing

We welcome contributions to this paper collection! To add a new paper:

1. Place it in the appropriate section based on topic
2. Include full citation with arXiv or publication link
3. Add a brief abstract or key contributions
4. Link to related projects and documentation
5. Follow the markdown formatting style of existing entries

For suggestions or additions, please open an issue or PR at
[pacoxu/AI-Infra](https://github.com/pacoxu/AI-Infra).
